name: Castillo de Montefrio -- circular via Tocon and Villanueva Mesia
name_original: Castillo de Montefrío  — circular por Tocón y Villanueva Mesía
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Montefrío are set within the Montes Occidentales, characterized by a diverse topography of rolling olive groves, rugged vegetation, and holm oak forests. The landscape features dramatic rock formations and cliffs, particularly in the Sierra de Chanzas and Sierra de Parapanda mountain ranges, which offer elevated viewpoints. Valleys and waterways, such as the Arroyo de los Molinos, further diversify the riding environments. This region provides varied terrain suitable for different touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Montefrío The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de Montefrio -- circular via Tocon and Villanueva Mesia, a difficult 28.4 miles (45.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Montefrío – Montefrío Castle loop from Huétor Tájar, a difficult 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the Montefrío Castle and surrounding olive groves. Local touring cyclists also love the Fuente de la Mora – Tower of Huétor-Tájar loop from Huétor Tájar, a moderate 20.3 miles (32.8 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and rolling hills, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Montefrío is defined by extensive olive groves, challenging mountain ranges, and dramatic rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Montefrío are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Montefrío's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Montefrío?
A: Montefrío offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 40 different options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging adventures.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Montefrío?
A: The terrain around Montefrío is incredibly diverse. You'll cycle through extensive rolling olive groves, rugged vegetation, and holm oak forests. Expect dramatic rock formations and cliffs, especially in the Sierra de Chanzas and Sierra de Parapanda mountain ranges, which provide elevated viewpoints. Valleys and waterways, such as the Arroyo de los Molinos, also add to the varied riding environments.

Q: Are there options for less experienced touring cyclists in Montefrío?
A: Yes, Montefrío offers several routes suitable for less experienced touring cyclists. There are 4 easy routes available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Fountain – Tower of Huétor-Tájar loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 21.6 km trail.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I explore along touring cycling routes in Montefrío?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to explore the region's rich history and natural beauty. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montefrío&lt;/a&gt; town itself, with its Moorish Fortress and Iglesia de la Villa perched on a rock. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Íllora&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the significant archaeological complex of Peña de los Gitanos.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints accessible by bike in Montefrío?
A: Absolutely. Montefrío is famous for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montefrío&lt;/a&gt; castle complex offers breathtaking panoramic views. The region is also home to the National Geographic Viewpoint, which provides the iconic vista of the town. Many routes, especially those in the Sierra de Chanzas and Sierra de Parapanda, offer elevated positions for spectacular scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Montefrío?
A: The touring cycling routes in Montefrío are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Over 200 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the extensive olive groves, challenging mountain ranges, and dramatic rock formations that define the region's unique cycling experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Montefrío?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Montefrío are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046832336/castillo-de-montefrio-circular-via-tocon-and-villanueva-mesia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montefrio -- circular via Tocon and Villanueva Mesia&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 45.6 km trail that offers significant elevation gain through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Montefrío is an excellent destination for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge. The region features 27 difficult routes with significant elevation gains, some exceeding 1,000 meters. These routes often include climbs like Alto de Montefrío, offering steady ascents through olive groves and forests. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21831142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isabel I of Castile Viewpoint – Tower of Huétor-Tájar loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 60.3 km route with over 1,200 meters of ascent.

Q: What are the typical distances for touring cycling routes in Montefrío?
A: The touring cycling routes in Montefrío vary significantly in distance. You can find shorter routes around 20 km, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31508947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Mora – Tower of Huétor-Tájar loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt; (32.8 km), up to longer, more demanding rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montefrío – Montefrío Castle loop from Huétor Tájar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53 km.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the famous olive groves?
A: Yes, cycling through the iconic olive groves is a quintessential Montefrío experience. Many routes are characterized by these rolling groves, some containing ancient trees over 500 years old. You'll find yourself immersed in this distinctive and beautiful agricultural landscape on numerous tours throughout the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Montefrío?
A: Montefrío offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, with comfortable weather and lush landscapes. While winter can be cooler, it's often mild enough for cycling, especially on sunny days. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: How well-maintained are the cycling surfaces in Montefrío?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Montefrío feature mostly well-paved surfaces, ensuring an enjoyable ride. The local authorities actively promote cycling tourism, contributing to the maintenance of routes. However, given the diverse terrain, some segments, particularly in more rugged areas or mountain biking trails, may be unpaved and more challenging.
tips: Located on the outskirts of town, the viewpoint offers a great view of Montefrío and its well-known rocky mountain where the impregnable castle from the Nasrid period stands.

Built in the mid-14th century, during the reign of Abu Abdallah Yusuf I, the castle is located on top of a spectacular rocky escarpment, in a strategic position that controls the valley of the Milanos River.

The Church of La Encarnación is one of the most prominent monuments in Montefrío, a town located in the region of Loja, in the province of Granada. This baroque-style church was built in the 18th century and stands out for its white stone façade, its spacious interior with three naves and its impressive main altarpiece made of carved and gilded wood. Furthermore, from its location on the hill, it offers spectacular views of the town and its surroundings.

waypoints.name: View of Montefrío and the Castle
